mnemonics: RMSprop: 'Resistant to Massive Parallelization Signals', 'Adam: Adaptive Moment estimation for Dynamic learning'
keyPoints: RMSprop: uses the exponentially decaying average of squared gradients to normalize the update rule, preventing exploding gradients.
Adam: combines the adaptability of momentum with the bias correction of RMSprop, giving more weight to recent gradients.
RMSprop: computationally less expensive than Adam due to fewer parameters to track, but may not perform as well on certain tasks.
Adam: handles non-stationarity by adapting the learning rate for each parameter, but may be more sensitive to hyperparameter tuning.
